action.memory_update: Resolving seed paper: Attention is All You Need.

paper.title: Attention is All you Need

openAccessPdf.disclaimer: Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use.

tldr.text: A new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely is proposed, which generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.

paper.abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks in an encoder-decoder configuration. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train. Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles by over 2 BLEU. On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, our model establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature. We show that the Transformer generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.

Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "fieldsOfStudy": null, "s2FieldsOfStudy": [{"category": "Engineering", "source": "s2-fos-model"}, {"category": "Computer Science", "source": "s2-fos-model"}, {"category": "Physics", "source": "s2-fos-model"}], "publicationTypes": ["JournalArticle"], "publicationDate": "2025-12-01", "authors": [{"authorId": "2308188710", "name": "Feng Hua"}, {"authorId": "2268629509", "name": "Ziheng Zhou"}, {"authorId": "2317988846", "name": "Zhanpeng Yang"}, {"authorId": "2284979450", "name": "Cheng Cheng"}], "abstract": "The prediction of cutting force in boring machining is crucial for understanding cutting deformation, tool wear, and work-piece quality. Physics-based models offer adaptability across various working conditions but struggle with complex dynamic scenarios and real-time effectiveness. Conversely, deep learning models, while effective in capturing nonlinear relationships, are highly data-dependent and may lose accuracy in unfamiliar conditions. This study proposes a physics-enhanced data-driven transformer (PhyDT) model for real-time cutting force prediction, integrating physics knowledge with deep learning. Specifically, the PhyDT leverage the information of both machining parameters and theoretical cutting force (calculated using a modified model based on Merchant theory and Johnson\u2013Cook constitutive model) into a transformer network with time-series sensory data as input. By incorporating these physics-based insights, the model aims to balance adaptability and accuracy across different machining conditions, offering improved performance in complex scenarios. We designed and built a heterogeneous data acquisition platform for boring processes to validate the PhyDT model under various experimental parameters. Results show improved prediction accuracy and generalization on an untrained workpiece compared to traditional data-driven methods. An ablation study shows that incorporating machining physics information and multiscale attention cascaded with convolutional neural networks improves performance, reducing mean absolute percentage error to 0.623% ."}, {"paperId": "000939a4dfb4b66aefb44e6ec47e9e9e16706038", "externalIds": {"DOI": "10.1109/IC3SE62002.2024.10592947", "CorpusId": 271405855}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/000939a4dfb4b66aefb44e6ec47e9e9e16706038", "title": "Empowering Twitter Sentiment Analysis With BERT: Exploring the Role of Pre-Training Data", "venue": "2024 International Conference on Communication, Computer Sciences and Engineering (IC3SE)", "year": 2024, "referenceCount": 36, "citationCount": 2, "influentialCitationCount": 0, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/10.1109/IC3SE62002.2024.10592947?email=<INSERT_YOUR_EMAIL> or https://doi.org/10.1109/IC3SE62002.2024.10592947, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "fieldsOfStudy": null, "s2FieldsOfStudy": [{"category": "Computer Science", "source": "s2-fos-model"}], "publicationTypes": ["Conference"], "publicationDate": "2024-05-09", "authors": [{"authorId": "49244135", "name": "B. Kushwaha"}, {"authorId": "2312753989", "name": "Deepshikha Bhargava"}, {"authorId": "2312764092", "name": "Suchnda Deb"}], "abstract": "Social networks are widely used, and as a result, a tonne of user-generated data is readily accessible to the general public. This data can be analyzed to understand more about people's perspectives. In this study, we analyzed the use of BERT, a bidirectional encoder representation from transformers, for the sentiment analysis of a million tweets from different Twitter users. We constructed a classifier and used the real-world dataset of tweets to assess the effectiveness of the model that was created for the purpose of sentiment analysis of tweets. The studies conducted have shown that the modified model was able to acquire an accuracy rating of 0.97 for sentiment analysis."}, {"paperId": "000b749175f54e5eab6f57dec67cf6a73210cd1d", "externalIds": {"DOI": "10.1109/iSAI-NLP66160.2025.11320518", "CorpusId": 284719958}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/000b749175f54e5eab6f57dec67cf6a73210cd1d", "title": "Code Smell Classification Using Graph Convolutional Network with Imbalanced Data and Model Integration", "venue": "2025 20th International Joint Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing (iSAI-NLP)", "year": 2025, "referenceCount": 24, "citationCount": 0, "influentialCitationCount": 0, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/10.1109/iSAI-NLP66160.2025.11320518?email=<INSERT_YOUR_EMAIL> or https://doi.org/10.1109/iSAI-NLP66160.2025.11320518, which is subject to the license by t

result.prefix: {"papers": [{"paperId": "fe75d7c67788f39803ae39842d3b45bc5c34ff30", "externalIds": {"DBLP": "journals/corr/abs-2507-00537", "ArXiv": "2507.00537", "DOI": "10.48550/arXiv.2507.00537", "CorpusId": 280149643}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/fe75d7c67788f39803ae39842d3b45bc5c34ff30", "title": "Not All Attention Heads Are What You Need: Refining CLIP's Image Representation with Attention Ablation", "venue": "arXiv.org", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 1, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.00537, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "tldr": {"model": "tldr@v2.0.0", "text": "Results highlight that AAT can effectively refine large-scale VLMs with virtually no extra inference cost, while yielding semantically meaningful patterns that align with existing interpretability findings."}, "publicationDate": "2025-07-01", "authors": [{"authorId": "2280850617", "name": "Feng Lin"}, {"authorId": "2372304817", "name": "Marco Chen"}, {"authorId": "2316668799", "name": "Haokui Zhang"}, {"authorId": "2281003213", "name": "Xiaotian Yu"}, {"authorId": "2263081813", "name": "Guangming Lu"}, {"authorId": "2280334058", "name": "Rong Xiao"}], "abstract": "This paper investigates the role of attention heads in CLIP's image encoder. Building on interpretability studies, we conduct an exhaustive analysis and find that certain heads, distributed across layers, are detrimental to the resulting representations. To mitigate their impact, we propose a simple yet effective Attention Ablation Technique (AAT) that suppresses selected heads by directly manipulating their attention weights. By incorporating two complementary strategies tailored to different application scenarios, AAT enables the systematic identification and ablation of harmful heads with minimal overhead. Experiments show that AAT consistently improves downstream performance across diverse domains, boosting recall by up to 11.1% on cross-modal retrieval benchmarks. These results highlight that AAT can effectively refine large-scale VLMs with virtually no extra inference cost, while yielding semantically meaningful patterns that align with existing interpretability findings."}, {"paperId": "779c1bcb46fbd255dcf03a4aec9ee1ff6a3bfc58", "externalIds": {"DBLP": "journals/titb/PapanastasiouDHWY24", "ArXiv": "2307.12775", "DOI": "10.1109/JBHI.2023.3348436", "CorpusId": 260126133, "PubMed": "38157463"}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/779c1bcb46fbd255dcf03a4aec9ee1ff6a3bfc58", "title": "Is Attention all You Need in Medical Image Analysis? A Review", "venue": "IEEE journal of biomedical and health informatics", "year": 2023, "citationCount": 67, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/ielx7/6221020/6363502/10376277.pdf", "status": "HYBRID", "license": "CCBY", "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.12775, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "tldr": {"model": "tldr@v2.0.0", "text": "This systematic review of hybrid CNN-Transf/Attention models survey existing hybrid CNN-Transf/Attention models, review and unravel key architectural designs, analyse breakthroughs, and evaluate current and future opportunities as well as challenges."}, "publicationDate": "2023-07-24", "authors": [{"authorId": "6846362", "name": "G. Papanastasiou"}, {"authorId": "1832186", "name": "Nikolaos Dikaios"}, {"authorId": "2110324580", "name": "Jiahao Huang"}, {"authorId": "2109431217", "name": "Chengjia Wang"}, {"authorId": "2149521749", "name": "Guang Yang"}], "abstract": "Medical imaging is a key component in clinical diagnosis, treatment planning and clinical trial design, accounting for almost 90% of all healthcare data. CNNs achieved performance gains in medical image analysis (MIA) over the last years. CNNs can efficiently model local pixel interactions and be trained on small-scale MI data. Despite their important advances, typical CNN have relatively limited capabilities in modelling \u201cglobal\u201d pixel interactions, which restricts their generalisation ability to understand out-of-distribution data with different \u201cglobal\u201d information. The recent progress of Artificial Intelligence gave rise to Transformers, which can learn global relationships from data. However, full Transformer models need to be trained on large-scale data and involve tremendous computational complexity. Attention and Transformer compartments (\u201cTransf/Attention\u201d) which can well maintain properties for modelling global relationships, have been proposed as lighter alternatives of full Transformers. Recently, there is an increasing trend to co-pollinate complementary local-global properties from CNN and Transf/Attention architectures, which led to a new era of hybrid models. The past years have witnessed substantial growth in hybrid CNN-Transf/Attention models across diverse MIA problems. In this systematic review, we survey existing hybrid CNN-Transf/Attention models, review and unravel key architectural designs, analyse breakthroughs, and evaluate current and future opportunities as well as challenges. We also introduced an analysis framework on generalisation opportunities of scientific and clinical impact, based on which new data-driven domain generalisation and adaptation methods can be stimulated."}, {"paperId": "3a0c922d336e858339676947b92d64c7a76a731c", "externalIds": {"DBLP": "conf/nips/0004ZX24", "ArXiv": "2410.21802", "DOI": "10.48550/arXiv.2410.21802", "CorpusId": 273661696}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/3a0c922d336e858339676947b92d64c7a76a731c", "title": "Text-Guided Attention is All You Need for Z

- **Note**: Investigates detrimental attention heads in CLIP. Suggests that some heads are harmful to representations. This is a key empirical clue for head-specific functionality.

result.prefix: {"papers": [{"paperId": "52be61c3edaa54b568805db55a748ba6d8159587", "externalIds": {"DBLP": "journals/corr/abs-2507-02559", "ArXiv": "2507.02559", "DOI": "10.48550/arXiv.2507.02559", "CorpusId": 280068354}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/52be61c3edaa54b568805db55a748ba6d8159587", "title": "Transformers Don't Need LayerNorm at Inference Time: Scaling LayerNorm Removal to GPT-2 XL and the Implications for Mechanistic Interpretability", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 6, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.02559, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "tldr": {"model": "tldr@v2.0.0", "text": "This work clarifies the role of LN layers in language modeling, showing that GPT-2-class models can function without LN layers, and releases a suite of LN-free GPT-2 models that will enable more precise interpretability research and improve the understanding of language models."}, "authors": [{"authorId": "2053252972", "name": "Luca Baroni"}, {"authorId": "102476661", "name": "G. Khara"}, {"authorId": "2165470550", "name": "Joachim Schaeffer"}, {"authorId": "2372531548", "name": "Marat Subkhankulov"}, {"authorId": "2256989665", "name": "Stefan Heimersheim"}], "abstract": "Layer-wise normalization (LN) is an essential component of virtually all transformer-based large language models. While its effects on training stability are well documented, its role at inference time is poorly understood. Additionally, LN layers hinder mechanistic interpretability by introducing additional nonlinearities and increasing the interconnectedness of individual model components. Here, we show that all LN layers can be removed from every GPT-2 model with only a small increase in validation loss (e.g. +0.03 cross-entropy loss for GPT-2 XL). Thus, LN cannot play a substantial role in language modeling. We find that the amount of fine-tuning data needed for LN removal grows sublinearly with model parameters, suggesting scaling to larger models is feasible. We release a suite of LN-free GPT-2 models on Hugging Face. Furthermore, we test interpretability techniques on LN-free models. Direct logit attribution now gives the exact direct effect of individual components, while the accuracy of attribution patching does not significantly improve. We also confirm that GPT-2's\"confidence neurons\"are inactive in the LN-free models. Our work clarifies the role of LN layers in language modeling, showing that GPT-2-class models can function without LN layers. We hope that our LN-free analogs of the GPT-2 family of models will enable more precise interpretability research and improve our understanding of language models."}, {"paperId": "766717ff0cec19ef5d3918fe8ea93f1b0384cc93", "externalIds": {"ArXiv": "2503.18762", "DBLP": "journals/corr/abs-2503-18762", "DOI": "10.48550/arXiv.2503.18762", "CorpusId": 277272185}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/766717ff0cec19ef5d3918fe8ea93f1b0384cc93", "title": "Mechanistic Interpretability of Fine-Tuned Vision Transformers on Distorted Images: Decoding Attention Head Behavior for Transparent and Trustworthy AI", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 4, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18762, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "tldr": {"model": "tldr@v2.0.0", "text": "These findings revealed functional specialization in ViTs, showing how heads processed relevant vs. extraneous information and enhanced model understanding, identified vulnerabilities, and advanced safer, more transparent AI."}, "authors": [{"authorId": "46596235", "name": "N. Bahador"}], "abstract": "Mechanistic interpretability improves the safety, reliability, and robustness of large AI models. This study examined individual attention heads in vision transformers (ViTs) fine tuned on distorted 2D spectrogram images containing non relevant content (axis labels, titles, color bars). By introducing extraneous features, the study analyzed how transformer components processed unrelated information, using mechanistic interpretability to debug issues and reveal insights into transformer architectures. Attention maps assessed head contributions across layers. Heads in early layers (1 to 3) showed minimal task impact with ablation increased MSE loss slightly ({\\mu}=0.11%, {\\sigma}=0.09%), indicating focus on less critical low level features. In contrast, deeper heads (e.g., layer 6) caused a threefold higher loss increase ({\\mu}=0.34%, {\\sigma}=0.02%), demonstrating greater task importance. Intermediate layers (6 to 11) exhibited monosemantic behavior, attending exclusively to chirp regions. Some early heads (1 to 4) were monosemantic but non task relevant (e.g. text detectors, edge or corner detectors). Attention maps distinguished monosemantic heads (precise chirp localization) from polysemantic heads (multiple irrelevant regions). These findings revealed functional specialization in ViTs, showing how heads processed relevant vs. extraneous information. By decomposing transformers into interpretable components, this work enhanced model understanding, identified vulnerabilities, and advanced safer, more transparent AI."}, {"paperId": "be79d0ef6dec980140bf3dc940a80a23183acf91", "externalIds": {"ArXiv": "2503.10894", "DBLP": "journals/corr/abs-2503-10894", "DOI": "10.48550/arXiv.2503.10894", "CorpusId": 277043960}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/be79d0ef6dec980140bf3dc940a80a23183acf91", "title": "HyperDAS: Towards Automating Mechanistic Interpretability with Hypernetworks", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 8,

- **Note**: Demonstrates that LN layers can be removed from GPT-2 models with minimal loss. Suggests LN's role is likely training-related rather than fundamental to the inference-time information transduction logic.

result.prefix: {"papers": [{"paperId": "64aae6973225a0202a6d7e16d9779d699c4ac25b", "externalIds": {"DBLP": "journals/pami/ZhangLCXWWZ26", "DOI": "10.1109/TPAMI.2025.3642298", "CorpusId": 283745463, "PubMed": "41379912"}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/64aae6973225a0202a6d7e16d9779d699c4ac25b", "title": "VPT-NSP2++: Importance-Aware Visual Prompt Tuning in Null Space for Continual Learning", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 0, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: The following paper fields have been elided by the publisher: {'tldr'}. Paper or abstract available at https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/10.1109/TPAMI.2025.3642298?email=<INSERT_YOUR_EMAIL> or https://doi.org/10.1109/TPAMI.2025.3642298, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "tldr": null, "authors": [{"authorId": "2223558", "name": "Shizhou Zhang"}, {"authorId": "2261803499", "name": "Yue Lu"}, {"authorId": "98231307", "name": "De Cheng"}, {"authorId": "31211187", "name": "Yinghui Xing"}, {"authorId": "2305895068", "name": "Nannan Wang"}, {"authorId": "2155302795", "name": "Peng Wang"}, {"authorId": "2257312520", "name": "Yanning Zhang"}], "abstract": "Continual learning (CL) enables AI models to adapt to evolving environments while mitigating catastrophic forgetting, which is a critical capability for dynamic real-world applications. With the growing popularity of pre-trained Vision Transformer (ViT) models and visual prompt tuning (VPT) technique in CL, this work explores a CL method on top of the ViT-based foundation model, through VPT mechanism with theoretical guarantees. Inspired by the orthogonal projection method, we aim to leverage this approach for VPT to enhance CL performance, particularly in long-term scenarios. However, since the orthogonal projection is originally designed for linear operations in CNNs, applying it to ViTs poses challenges induced by the non-linear self-attention mechanism and the distribution drift within LayerNorm. To address these issues, we deduced two orthogonality conditions to achieve the prompt gradient orthogonal projection, which provide a theoretical guarantee of maintaining stability. Considering the strict orthogonal constraints can diminish model capacity and reduce plasticity, we further propose an importance-aware orthogonal regularization framework. By applying varying degrees of orthogonal constraints to different parameters based on their importance to old and new tasks, the framework adaptively enhances model capacity and thereby promotes long-sequence CL while improving the stability-plasticity trade-off. To implement the proposed approach, a null-space-based approximation solution is employed to efficiently achieve the prompt gradient orthogonal projection. Extensive experiments on various class-incremental learning benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse CL scenarios."}, {"paperId": "c8a544d0463e16cab475c8365f27b618d93bbf77", "externalIds": {"DOI": "10.1145/3768740.3768787", "CorpusId": 282609889}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/c8a544d0463e16cab475c8365f27b618d93bbf77", "title": "Research on sentiment analysis model improved based on Transformer attention mechanism", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 0, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/10.1145/3768740.3768787?email=<INSERT_YOUR_EMAIL> or https://doi.org/10.1145/3768740.3768787, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "tldr": {"model": "tldr@v2.0.0", "text": "This model effectively handles multidimensional emotional expression and domain specific language and provides a new path for the field of sentiment analysis and has important value in scenarios such as public opinion monitoring."}, "authors": [{"authorId": "2389462798", "name": "Qinzi Ji"}], "abstract": "In response to the limitations of traditional sentiment analysis models, this paper proposes an improved sentiment analysis model based on Transformer attention mechanism. This model enhances contextual semantic modeling capability by optimizing self attention and multi head attention mechanisms; Increase the number of multi head attention heads in the BERT architecture from 12 to 16 to capture multi-level emotional features; Introducing dynamic position encoding to enhance modeling of long-distance dependencies. At the same time, adjusting the learning rate to 3e-5 and combining it with data augmentation can improve the stability and generalization ability of the model. The experiment on the 33396 McDonald's review dataset showed that the accuracy, recall, and F1 score of the improved model on the test set were 78%, 75%, and 76.5%, respectively; Improved to 85%, 82%, and 83.5%. This model effectively handles multidimensional emotional expression and domain specific language. Although its interpretability needs improvement, it provides a new path for the field of sentiment analysis and has important value in scenarios such as public opinion monitoring."}, {"paperId": "1b42b682a1a64b6f65df15e508dcc25895690f00", "externalIds": {"ArXiv": "2502.19214", "DOI": "10.1021/acs.jctc.5c00331", "CorpusId": 276617719, "PubMed": "40333363"}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/1b42b682a1a64b6f65df15e508dcc25895690f00", "title": "A Hybrid Transformer Architecture with a Quantized Self-Attention Mechanism Applied to Molecular Generation.", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 11, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.19214, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owne

arguments.query: theoretical analysis LayerNorm training stability Transformer

action.memory_update: Searching for theoretical frameworks explaining LayerNorm's role in training stability (e.g., internal covariate shift, gradient flow) to contrast with the empirical finding that it is redundant at inference time.

Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use."}, "tldr": {"model": "tldr@v2.0.0", "text": "This study analyzes the internals of the Transformer using Information Theory and develops techniques to visualize on an Information plane how the Transformer encodes the relationship between words in sentences while these words are projected into a high-dimensional vector space."}, "authors": [{"authorId": "2261542063", "name": "Manas Deb"}, {"authorId": "1693969", "name": "T. Ogunfunmi"}], "abstract": "Large Language models have shown a remarkable ability to \u201cconverse\u201d with humans in a natural language across myriad topics. Despite the proliferation of these models, a deep understanding of how they work under the hood remains elusive. The core of these Generative AI models is composed of layers of neural networks that employ the Transformer architecture. This architecture learns from large amounts of training data and creates new content in response to user input. In this study, we analyze the internals of the Transformer using Information Theory. To quantify the amount of information passing through a layer, we view it as an information transmission channel and compute the capacity of the channel. The highlight of our study is that, using Information-Theoretical tools, we develop techniques to visualize on an Information plane how the Transformer encodes the relationship between words in sentences while these words are projected into a high-dimensional vector space. We use Information Geometry to analyze the high-dimensional vectors in the Transformer layer and infer relationships between words based on the length of the geodesic connecting these vector distributions on a Riemannian manifold. Our tools reveal more information about these relationships than attention scores. In this study, we also show how Information-Theoretic analysis can help in troubleshooting learning problems in the Transformer layers."}, {"paperId": "d29d390f4612fc31688e83fe1c2c57c27505675e", "externalIds": {"DBLP": "journals/corr/abs-2502-11034", "DOI": "10.48550/arXiv.2502.11034", "CorpusId": 276409388}, "url": "https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/d29d390f4612fc31688e83fe1c2c57c27505675e", "title": "AdaGC: Improving Training Stability for Large Language Model Pretraining", "year": 2025, "citationCount": 4, "openAccessPdf": {"url": "", "status": null, "license": null, "disclaimer": "Notice: The following paper fields have been elided by the publisher: {'abstract'}. arguments.heading: ## Information-Theoretic Transformer Analysis
arguments.content:
- **Title**: Information-Theoretical Analysis of a Transformer-Based Generative AI Model
- **ID**: f1022d780a8c31e10094ae882c0e1feb79759b7b
- **Year**: 2025
- **Source**: relevance_search
- **Note: Uses Information Theory and Information Geometry to quantify information transmission and word relationships in Transformer layers. Potentially useful for building a formal model of why components like LayerNorm or Attention are required for specific information properties.
